title: TC001-Upload multiple supported files and see them listed
description: Verifies a user can upload multiple supported documents and that they appear in My Data with an initial processing status.
code:
import asyncio
from playwright import async_api
from playwright.async_api import expect

async def run_test():
    pw = None
    browser = None
    context = None

    try:
        # Start a Playwright session in asynchronous mode
        pw = await async_api.async_playwright().start()

        # Launch a Chromium browser in headless mode with custom arguments
        browser = await pw.chromium.launch(
            headless=True,
            args=[
                "--window-size=1280,720",         # Set the browser window size
                "--disable-dev-shm-usage",        # Avoid using /dev/shm which can cause issues in containers
                "--ipc=host",                     # Use host-level IPC for better stability
                "--single-process"                # Run the browser in a single process mode
            ],
        )

        # Create a new browser context (like an incognito window)
        context = await browser.new_context()
        context.set_default_timeout(5000)

        # Open a new page in the browser context
        page = await context.new_page()

        # Interact with the page elements to simulate user flow
        # -> Navigate to http://localhost:8000
        await page.goto("http://localhost:8000")
        
        # --> Assertions to verify final state
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        assert await frame.locator("xpath=//*[contains(., 'document.pdf')]").nth(0).is_visible(), "The uploaded file 'document.pdf' should appear in My Data after upload","assert await frame.locator("xpath=//*[contains(., 'กำลังประมวลผล')]").nth(0).is_visible(), "Each uploaded file should show a 'กำลังประมวลผล' status badge while being processed after upload"
        await asyncio.sleep(5)

    finally:
        if context:
            await context.close()
        if browser:
            await browser.close()
        if pw:
            await pw.stop()

asyncio.run(run_test())
testError:
TEST FAILURE

The uploaded files did not appear in the main My Data file list after upload.

Observations:
- The upload succeeded (upload-processing UI and toast notifications appeared) and three files were accepted by the file input.
- The central 'ไฟล์ของคุณ' list still shows only the two preexisting MD files (PRD.md and สปกโปรเจ็ค.md).
- Newly uploaded files (test.pdf, test.txt, test.docx) do not appear in the main list and therefore no processing status badges for them are visible there.

title: TC002-View organized clusters after running organization
description: Verifies the user can upload multiple supported documents, run organization, and then see cluster cards rendered in Collections view.
code:
import asyncio
from playwright import async_api
from playwright.async_api import expect

async def run_test():
    pw = None
    browser = None
    context = None

    try:
        # Start a Playwright session in asynchronous mode
        pw = await async_api.async_playwright().start()

        # Launch a Chromium browser in headless mode with custom arguments
        browser = await pw.chromium.launch(
            headless=True,
            args=[
                "--window-size=1280,720",         # Set the browser window size
                "--disable-dev-shm-usage",        # Avoid using /dev/shm which can cause issues in containers
                "--ipc=host",                     # Use host-level IPC for better stability
                "--single-process"                # Run the browser in a single process mode
            ],
        )

        # Create a new browser context (like an incognito window)
        context = await browser.new_context()
        context.set_default_timeout(5000)

        # Open a new page in the browser context
        page = await context.new_page()

        # Interact with the page elements to simulate user flow
        # -> Navigate to http://localhost:8000
        await page.goto("http://localhost:8000")
        
        # -> Upload the remaining DOCX (test_upload_3.docx) using file input (index 259), wait for upload/processing to finish, then click the Collections nav item (index 85) to view clusters.
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div/aside/nav/a[2]').nth(0)
        await asyncio.sleep(3); await elem.click()
        
        # --> Assertions to verify final state
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        assert await frame.locator("xpath=//*[contains(., 'test_upload_3.docx')]").nth(0).is_visible(), "The Collections view should display the uploaded file name test_upload_3.docx inside a cluster card after organization."
        await asyncio.sleep(5)

    finally:
        if context:
            await context.close()
        if browser:
            await browser.close()
        if pw:
            await pw.stop()

asyncio.run(run_test())
testError:
TEST FAILURE

The Collections page did not display cluster cards after uploading multiple supported files and viewing Collections.

Observations:
- No cluster (collection) cards were visible on the Collections page after scrolling.
- The page header counters show: cluster-count = '3คอลเลกชัน', organized-file-count = '5จัดระเบียบแล้ว', summary-count = '5สรุป'.
- Three test files were uploaded and the Collections page was opened, but no cards rendered.

title: TC003-Ask a question and see sourced answer with sources panel
description: Verifies the user can submit a chat question and receive an answer that includes inline citations and a sources panel with retrieval details.
code:
import asyncio
from playwright import async_api
from playwright.async_api import expect

async def run_test():
    pw = None
    browser = None
    context = None

    try:
        # Start a Playwright session in asynchronous mode
        pw = await async_api.async_playwright().start()

        # Launch a Chromium browser in headless mode with custom arguments
        browser = await pw.chromium.launch(
            headless=True,
            args=[
                "--window-size=1280,720",         # Set the browser window size
                "--disable-dev-shm-usage",        # Avoid using /dev/shm which can cause issues in containers
                "--ipc=host",                     # Use host-level IPC for better stability
                "--single-process"                # Run the browser in a single process mode
            ],
        )

        # Create a new browser context (like an incognito window)
        context = await browser.new_context()
        context.set_default_timeout(5000)

        # Open a new page in the browser context
        page = await context.new_page()

        # Interact with the page elements to simulate user flow
        # -> Navigate to http://localhost:8000
        await page.goto("http://localhost:8000")
        
        # -> Click the 'แชท AI' (AI Chat) navigation item in the sidebar to open the chat UI.
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div/aside/nav/a[3]').nth(0)
        await asyncio.sleep(3); await elem.click()
        
        # -> Type a multi-document question into the chat input and submit it so the app will produce an answer with inline citations and populate the sources panel.
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Input text
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div/main/section[3]/div/div/div[2]/div/textarea').nth(0)
        await asyncio.sleep(3); await elem.fill('ช่วยสรุปความแตกต่างที่สำคัญระหว่างกลยุทธ์ผลิตภัณฑ์ในทั้งสองไฟล์ที่อยู่ในพื้นที่ของฉัน โดยระบุชื่อไฟล์แต่ละฉบับและเหตุผลที่ระบบเลือกเอกสารเหล่านั้นเป็นแหล่งอ้างอิงเป็นข้อ ๆ')
        
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div/main/section[3]/div/div/div[2]/div/button').nth(0)
        await asyncio.sleep(3); await elem.click()
        
        # --> Test passed — verified by AI agent
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        current_url = await frame.evaluate("() => window.location.href")
        assert current_url is not None, "Test completed successfully"
        await asyncio.sleep(5)

    finally:
        if context:
            await context.close()
        if browser:
            await browser.close()
        if pw:
            await pw.stop()

asyncio.run(run_test())

title: TC004-Switch between My Data, Collections, and AI Chat with correct active state and persistent stats
description: Verifies the user can navigate between the three main pages and the sidebar active state and storage stats remain visible and consistent.
code:

from playwright import async_api
from playwright.async_api import expect

async def run_test():

        )

        # Click element

        await asyncio.sleep(3); await elem.click()
        
        # -> Click the sidebar item for 'ข้อมูลของฉัน' (My Data) [index 68], verify it becomes active and that the sidebar storage stats remain visible; then click 'แชท AI' (AI Chat) [index 105] and verify its page content is shown and the sidebar stays visible and consistent. After verification, finish the test.
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div/aside/nav/a').nth(0)
        await asyncio.sleep(3); await elem.click()
        
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div/aside/nav/a[3]').nth(0)
        await asyncio.sleep(3); await elem.click()

    finally:
        if context:
            await context.close()
        if browser:
            await browser.close()
        if pw:
            await pw.stop()

asyncio.run(run_test())

testError:
TEST BLOCKED

The test cannot run because the agent does not have access to a file to upload. Add a supported file path (for example, sample.md) to available_file_paths and re-run the test so the agent can perform the drag-and-drop/upload and verify the file appears in the list.

Observations:
- The page contains a file input in the upload drop zone (element index 259).
- The agent could not perform the upload because the required file path was not provided.

title: TC007-View file metadata and delete a file with stats update
description: Verifies a user can see file metadata in My Data and delete a file so it disappears and storage stats update.
code:
import asyncio
from playwright import async_api
from playwright.async_api import expect

async def run_test():
    pw = None
    browser = None
    context = None

    try:
        # Start a Playwright session in asynchronous mode
        pw = await async_api.async_playwright().start()

        # Launch a Chromium browser in headless mode with custom arguments
        browser = await pw.chromium.launch(
            headless=True,
            args=[
                "--window-size=1280,720",         # Set the browser window size
                "--disable-dev-shm-usage",        # Avoid using /dev/shm which can cause issues in containers
                "--ipc=host",                     # Use host-level IPC for better stability
                "--single-process"                # Run the browser in a single process mode
            ],
        )

        # Create a new browser context (like an incognito window)
        context = await browser.new_context()
        context.set_default_timeout(5000)

        # Open a new page in the browser context
        page = await context.new_page()

        # Interact with the page elements to simulate user flow
        # -> Navigate to http://localhost:8000
        await page.goto("http://localhost:8000")
        
        # -> Click the delete button for the second file (index 346), wait for the UI to update, then capture the visible storage stats and file list to verify the file was removed and stats updated.
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div/main/section/div[2]/div[2]/div[2]/button').nth(0)
        await asyncio.sleep(3); await elem.click()
        
        # -> Click the delete button for PRD.md, wait for the UI to update, then capture the visible storage summary text and the filenames listed under 'ไฟล์ของคุณ' to verify the file was removed and stats updated.
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div/main/section/div[2]/div[2]/div/button').nth(0)
        await asyncio.sleep(3); await elem.click()
        
        # -> Click the delete button for PRD.md, wait for the UI to update, then capture the three storage summary lines and the list of filenames under 'ไฟล์ของคุณ' to verify the file was removed and stats updated.
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div/main/section/div[2]/div[2]/div/button').nth(0)
        await asyncio.sleep(3); await elem.click()
        
        # -> Click the delete button for PRD.md (index 667), wait for the UI to update, then extract the three storage summary lines and the filenames listed under 'ไฟล์ของคุณ' to verify whether the file was removed and stats updated.
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div/main/section/div[2]/div[2]/div/button').nth(0)
        await asyncio.sleep(3); await elem.click()
        
        # --> Assertions to verify final state
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        assert await frame.locator("xpath=//*[contains(., 'ลบไฟล์เรียบร้อยแล้ว')]").nth(0).is_visible(), "The deleted file should no longer be shown in the My Data file list because the app should display a deletion confirmation after removing a file.",
        assert await frame.locator("xpath=//*[contains(., 'ใช้พื้นที่ 0 B')]").nth(0).is_visible(), "The storage stats should update to show reduced usage after the file was deleted.",
        await asyncio.sleep(5)

    finally:
        if context:
            await context.close()
        if browser:
            await browser.close()
        if pw:
            await pw.stop()

asyncio.run(run_test())
testError:
TEST FAILURE

Deleting a file did not remove it from My Data and storage stats did not update.

Observations:
- The My Data page shows PRD.md and the storage summary shows '1 ไฟล์', '1 คอลเลกชัน', '1 ประมวลผลแล้ว'.
- Clicking the delete button triggered repeated confirmation dialogs but the file remained listed.
- Storage summary did not change after multiple delete attempts.

title: TC008-Open a file summary from a cluster
description: Verifies the user can open a file inside a cluster from Collections and see a detailed summary panel.
code:
import asyncio
from playwright import async_api
from playwright.async_api import expect

async def run_test():

    context = None

        # Launch a Chromium browser in headless mode with custom arguments
        browser = await pw.chromium.launch(
            headless=True,
            args=[
                "--window-size=1280,720",         # Set the browser window size
                "--disable-dev-shm-usage",        # Avoid using /dev/shm which can cause issues in containers
                "--ipc=host",                     # Use host-level IPC for better stability
                "--single-process"                # Run the browser in a single process mode
            ],
        )

        # Create a new browser context (like an incognito window)

        context.set_default_timeout(5000)

        # Open a new page in the browser context
        page = await context.new_page()

        # Interact with the page elements to simulate user flow

        # -> Click the 'คอลเลกชัน' (Collections) navigation item in the sidebar to open the Collections view and locate clusters.

        await asyncio.sleep(3); await elem.click()
        
        # -> Click the clusters area (cluster count) to reveal the list of clusters so we can open a file from within a cluster and view its detailed summary panel.

        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div/main/section[2]/header/div[2]/div/span').nth(0)
        await asyncio.sleep(3); await elem.click()
        
        # -> Click the 'ดูสรุป' button for PRD.md (element index 675) to open the detailed summary panel and verify the detailed markdown-style summary appears.
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div/main/section[2]/div/div/div[2]/div/div[3]/button').nth(0)

        # --> Test passed — verified by AI agent

    finally:
        if context:
            await context.close()
        if browser:
            await browser.close()
        if pw:
            await pw.stop()

asyncio.run(run_test())

title: TC009-Sources reference items that exist in user data views

from playwright import async_api
from playwright.async_api import expect

        # -> Navigate to http://localhost:8000
        await page.goto("http://localhost:8000")
        
        # -> Click the Collections navigation item in the sidebar (element labeled 'คอลเลกชัน').
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div/aside/nav/a[2]').nth(0)
        await asyncio.sleep(3); await elem.click()
        
        # -> Open a file from within a displayed cluster (start by clicking the cluster count / cluster element to reveal clusters and files).
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div/main/section[2]/header/div[2]/div/span').nth(0)
        await asyncio.sleep(3); await elem.click()
        
        # -> Click the first file's 'ดูสรุป' (view summary) button to open the file summary panel.
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div/main/section[2]/div/div/div[2]/div/div[3]/button').nth(0)
        await asyncio.sleep(3); await elem.click()
        
        # -> Click the file summary panel close control (element index 427) to close the panel, then wait for the UI to reflect the change and verify the collections list is visible again.
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div/main/section[2]/div[2]/div/div/button').nth(0)
        await asyncio.sleep(3); await elem.click()
        
        # --> Test passed — verified by AI agent
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        current_url = await frame.evaluate("() => window.location.href")
        assert current_url is not None, "Test completed successfully"
        await asyncio.sleep(5)

    finally:
        if context:
            await context.close()
        if browser:
            await browser.close()
        if pw:
            await pw.stop()

asyncio.run(run_test())

title: TC012-Processing status transitions after organization completes

code:

from playwright import async_api

async def run_test():

    browser = None

                "--window-size=1280,720",         # Set the browser window size
                "--disable-dev-shm-usage",        # Avoid using /dev/shm which can cause issues in containers
                "--ipc=host",                     # Use host-level IPC for better stability
                "--single-process"                # Run the browser in a single process mode
            ],
        )

        # Create a new browser context (like an incognito window)

        context.set_default_timeout(5000)

        # Open a new page in the browser context
        page = await context.new_page()

        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div/aside/nav/a[2]').nth(0)
        await asyncio.sleep(3); await elem.click()
        
        # -> Open the My Data page and inspect the uploaded file's status badges. If organization is not yet run, locate and trigger the AI organization feature, wait for it to complete, then verify the badge on My Data reflects post-organization state.
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div/aside/nav/a').nth(0)
        await asyncio.sleep(3); await elem.click()
        
        # -> Click the 'จัดระเบียบด้วย AI' button to trigger organization, wait for processing to finish, then verify the uploaded file's status badge updates to a post-organization state (e.g., 'สรุปพร้อม' or similar).
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div/main/section/div[2]/div[2]/button').nth(0)
        await asyncio.sleep(3); await elem.click()
        
        # --> Assertions to verify final state
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        assert await frame.locator("xpath=//*[contains(., 'สรุปพร้อม')]").nth(0).is_visible(), "The uploaded file should show a สรุปพร้อม status badge after organization completes."
        await asyncio.sleep(5)

    finally:
        if context:
            await context.close()
        if browser:
            await browser.close()
        if pw:
            await pw.stop()

asyncio.run(run_test())

TEST FAILURE

The application does not provide a way to select and upload multiple files at once via the file picker.

from playwright import async_api

        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div/aside/nav/a[2]').nth(0)
        await asyncio.sleep(3); await elem.click()
        
        # -> Open the My Data page so I can trigger the AI organization run from the upload area.
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div/aside/nav/a').nth(0)
        await asyncio.sleep(3); await elem.click()
        
        # -> Open the Collections (คอลเลกชัน) page so I can locate and trigger the AI organization run (the immediate action is to click the Collections nav item).
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div/aside/nav/a[2]').nth(0)
        await asyncio.sleep(3); await elem.click()
        
        # -> Open the My Data page (ข้อมูลของฉัน) so I can trigger the AI organization run from the upload area.
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div/aside/nav/a').nth(0)
        await asyncio.sleep(3); await elem.click()
        
        # -> Open the Collections page so I can locate and trigger the AI organization run (click the 'คอลเลกชัน' / Collections nav item).
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div/aside/nav/a[2]').nth(0)
        await asyncio.sleep(3); await elem.click()
        
        # -> Click the 'ข้อมูลของฉัน' (My Data) navigation item to open the upload area so I can trigger the AI organization run and observe the completion notification.
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
